summary: Maintenance is reorganized into Clean My Mac, Developer Projects, and System Cleanup; Disk Analysis and Duplicates Finder get sticky headers; duplicate scans can be cancelled; and false low-space alerts on tiny mounts are fixed.
introduced: Clean My Mac sidebar section — App Caches, Browser Caches, Developer Caches, Logs, Temporary Files, Trash, and Installers each open directly from the sidebar with section descriptions
Developer Projects sidebar section — node_modules, Build Artifacts, and Virtual Environments as first-class menu items for reclaiming heavy project folders
System Cleanup sidebar section — APFS Snapshots, Uninstall Apps (with icon), and Optimize moved out of the old System Maintenance split view
Disk Analysis header — sticky title, drive picker, free-space badge, and scan actions matching System Optimization layout
Duplicates Finder header — Find Duplicates, Scan Again, and Cancel controls live in the pane instead of the sidebar Actions menu
changed: System Maintenance removed — cleanup tools are now grouped under three dedicated sidebar sections with sub-menus and footer descriptions
Duplicate detection — start and cancel scans only from Duplicates Finder; sidebar Actions links to Review Duplicates without auto-starting a scan
Disk Analysis toolbar — volume picker and scan buttons moved into the section header for a cleaner main toolbar
Maintenance detail panels — full-width layout with sticky section headers (Clean My Mac, Developer Projects, or System Cleanup) above each tool
fixed: Disk space notifications no longer fire for tiny mounts such as app disk images — RAM-based critical alerts apply only to the system disk, and volumes under 512 MB are ignored
